clap.def: 拍手、鼓掌、雷聲、霹靂聲的人物 (單數)
clap.col: clap one's hands, clap of thunder, clap eyes on
clap.ex: Please clap your hands together for our young performers.

clapper.def: 鈴舌、拍手者的人物 (單數)
clapper.ex: The massive iron clapper hit the side of the bell loudly.

clapping.ex: There was enthusiastic clapping at the end of the concert.

claptrap.def: 嘩眾取寵的話、胡說八道人物 (單數)
claptrap.ex: His political speech was full of pure and simple claptrap.

claqe/claque.def: 僱傭喝采者、集團的人物 (單數)
claqe/claque.ex: The performer hired a small claque to cheer for him today.

clarence.def: 四輪閉鎖式馬車人物 (單數)
clarence.ex: The horse-drawn clarence waited quietly by the palace gate.

claret.def: 波爾多紅酒、深紅色的人物 (單數)
claret.ex: He enjoyed a cool glass of claret with his evening meal.

clarification.ex: We need further clarification on several points of the law.

clarified.def: 澄清了的、已淨化的人的人 (或物)
clarified.ex: She used clarified butter to cook the delicate white fish.

clarify.def: 澄清、說明、使純潔的人的人
clarify.ex: The latest report helped to clarify the current situation.

clarinet.ex: She has been practicing the clarinet for three hours today.

clarinettist.ex: The lead clarinettist played a stunning solo with orchestra.

clarion.ex: His speech was a clarion call for social justice and peace.

clarity.def: 清楚、清晰、透明的人物 (單數)
clarity.ex: The crystalline clarity of the mountain lake was amazing.

clash.def: 衝突、不協調、鏗鏘聲的人物 (單數)
clash.col: culture clash, personality clash, clash of interests
clash.ex: There was a sudden clash of cultures in the small village.

clashing.def: 衝突的、不協調的的人 (或物)
clashing.ex: She was wearing clashing colors that hurt my eyes tonight.

clasp.def: 緊握、抱、扣住、鉤子的人物 (單數)
clasp.ex: He gave his old friend a very warm and tight hand clasp.

clasped.def: 緊握了的、扣住的人的人 (或物)
clasped.ex: She sat quietly with her clasped hands in her lap tonight.

clasper.def: 緊握者、(動)鰭腳人物 (單數)
clasper.ex: The vine used its tiny tendril clasper to climb the wall.

class.def: 班級、等級、課、階級的人物 (單數)
class.col: middle class, working class, first class
class.ex: She is the brightest student in our entire history class.

classic.def: 經典的、典型的、經典作品的人物 (經)
classic.col: classic example, cult classic, classic literature
classic.ex: This movie has become a cult classic over the many years.

classical.def: 古典的、經典的人的人物
classical.col: classical music, classical education, classical studies
classical.ex: She loves listening to classical music while she works.

classically.ex: He is a classically trained pianist from the local academy.

classicism.def: 古典主義、古典風格人物 (單數)
classicism.ex: The grand building is a fine example of pure classicism.

classicist.def: 古典主義者、古典學者 (單數)
classicist.ex: The leading classicist translated many ancient Greek plays.

classics.def: 古典文學、古典名著的人物 (複數)
classics.ex: He spent years studying the classics at Oxford University.
